title: Zoho Sign API Vocabulary
description: Domain vocabulary and terminology for the Zoho Sign electronic signature API

terms.definition: A document or set of documents sent for signature collection. Also called an envelope. Contains recipients, signing fields, and tracks the overall signing status.
A signing task assigned to a specific recipient within a request. Each action has a type (SIGN, VIEW, APPROVE, INPERSONSIGN) and tracks the recipient's progress.
A reusable document configuration with predefined fields and recipient roles that can be used to generate new signing requests without re-uploading documents.
A designated area in a document where a recipient must provide input, such as a signature, initials, date, name, or other text information.
A person who receives a document for signing or viewing. Identified by name and email address and assigned a specific action type.
A signing workflow where recipients must sign in a specified order. The next recipient only receives the document after the previous one completes their action.
A signing workflow where all recipients receive the document simultaneously and can sign in any order.
A chronological record of all actions taken on a document, including who viewed, signed, or declined it and when, used for legal compliance.
A signing experience embedded directly within a third-party application via an iframe or redirect, allowing signers to sign without leaving the host application.
A signing workflow where a facilitator collects signatures from signers who are physically present, using the same device.
Qualified Electronic Signature — the highest level of electronic signature under the EU eIDAS regulation, with the same legal standing as a handwritten signature.
FDA regulation governing electronic records and electronic signatures in the pharmaceutical and life sciences industries. Zoho Sign supports compliant workflows.

terms.description: The authentication protocol used by Zoho Sign API. Requires obtaining access tokens via Zoho Accounts using authorization code or self-client flows.

terms.definition: A feature to send the same document to multiple recipients simultaneously, creating individual requests for each recipient from a single action.
The action of canceling or revoking an in-progress document request before all recipients have completed their actions.
